---
title: "Evidence-Based Engineering"
subtitle: "Applied statistics, environmental engineering, and reproducible analysis"
---

# Vince Pileggi, Ph.D., P.Eng.

## Evidence-Based Engineering

I develop and share educational resources, research notes, and reproducible methods at the intersection of environmental engineering, applied statistics, and technical communication.

This site focuses on public, non-confidential material related to engineering statistics, wastewater research, teaching, publications, and reproducible workflows. It does not contain internal reviews, regulatory assessments, or unpublished work prepared through my government employment.

::: {.callout-note}
Material on this site reflects my academic and professional interests, including my adjunct professor role at the University of Guelph. It does not represent the views, policies, or positions of my employer.
:::

## Current Focus

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### Engineering Statistics

Practical statistical methods for environmental and civil engineering, including reliability analysis, uncertainty, visualization, and reproducible reporting.

[Explore methods](research.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Wastewater Research

Public research and educational material related to wastewater treatment, environmental monitoring, and data-driven engineering assessment.

[View research](reports.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Teaching and Mentoring

Resources for students, engineers, and early-career professionals interested in applying statistics and reproducible workflows to engineering problems.

[Teaching resources](services.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### Reproducible Workflows

Examples using Quarto, R, Python, GitHub, and open technical writing tools for transparent engineering analysis and communication.

[Learn more](technology-framework.qmd)
:::

:::

## Guiding Principle

> Engineering decisions are strongest when they are supported by transparent evidence, reproducible analysis, and clear communication of uncertainty.

## Site Roadmap

This site will gradually include:

- public research and publications;
- engineering statistics tutorials;
- wastewater engineering teaching examples;
- reproducible Quarto, R, and Python workflows;
- public presentations and academic material;
- selected non-confidential technical notes.
